seo.title: Colorado Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Colorado — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license colorado, dental assistant certification colorado, RDA colorado, DANB CDA colorado, dental assistant radiography colorado

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: N/A - No general license or renewal
quick_facts.processing_time: N/A - No general license or application process

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in Colorado.

steps.title: Understand Colorado Dental Assistant Regulations
steps.description: Familiarize yourself with the Colorado Dental Board's statutes and rules concerning dental assistants, particularly Rule 1.16 on Expanded Duties. General dental assisting duties do not require a license, but are performed under a dentist's supervision.

steps.title: Identify Required Training for Expanded Duties
steps.description: If you plan to perform expanded duties such as radiography, coronal polishing, or pit and fissure sealants, identify the specific training or examination requirements outlined by the Colorado Dental Board. These are qualifications you must possess, not an application process via a portal.

steps.title: Complete Board-Approved Education or Examination
steps.description: Successfully complete a course of study approved by the Colorado Dental Board or pass a board-approved examination for each specific expanded duty you intend to perform. These courses are offered by educational institutions or testing bodies, not directly by the board.

steps.title: Maintain Documentation of Qualifications
steps.description: Keep thorough records of all completed board-approved training, certifications, and examinations. Your supervising dentist is responsible for ensuring you meet all requirements for delegated duties, and this documentation may be requested during an audit.

steps.title: Work Under Direct Supervision
steps.description: Ensure you always perform duties under the direct supervision of a licensed Colorado dentist, as required by state law. The dentist is ultimately responsible for your delegated functions.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Colorado does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does Colorado require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, Colorado does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Colorado?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Colorado.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Colorado?
faqs.answer: Radiography requirements vary — check with the state board.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Colorado?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Colorado. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.processing_time: N/A - No general license or application process
reciprocity.endorsement_url: N/A - No general license or endorsement process
reciprocity.notes: Colorado does not license or register general dental assistants. Expanded duty registration may apply.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Colorado
hero.dek: Colorado does not license general dental assistants. Dental assistants operate under the direct supervision of a licensed dentist, but specific expanded duties such as radiography or coronal polishing require completion of board-approved training or exams, overseen by the Colorado Dental Board.

official_links.portal_name: Colorado Dental Board Information (No General DA License Application)
official_links.registration_steps: The Colorado Dental Board does not license general dental assistants. There is no central online application system for a DA license. Information regarding requirements for specific expanded duties can be found on the board's website.
